report_name: UK NCSC: Hostile States Behind 75% of Attacks on Critical Infrastructure

threat_actors.name: State-sponsored actors of Russia
threat_actors.description: Russian state-sponsored actors identified by the UK NCSC as actively 'prepositioning' within UK critical national infrastructure networks to establish dormant footholds for potential future disruption.

threat_actors.name: State-sponsored actors of China
threat_actors.description: Chinese state-sponsored actors, including groups like Volt Typhoon, identified by the UK NCSC as actively 'prepositioning' within UK critical national infrastructure networks using living-off-the-land techniques.

threat_actors.name: State-sponsored actors of Iran
threat_actors.description: Iranian state-sponsored actors identified by the UK NCSC as actively 'prepositioning' within UK critical national infrastructure networks.

threat_actors.name: Volt Typhoon
threat_actors.description: A China-linked group cited by the NCSC as an example of the type of threat facing the UK, known for living-off-the-land techniques and prepositioning in critical infrastructure.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: Hostile state actors establish dormant footholds in CNI networks, implying the use of compromised or created valid accounts for persistence and prepositioning.

attack_patterns.name: External Remote Services
attack_patterns.description: State-sponsored actors likely gain initial access to CNI networks by exploiting external remote services to begin their 'prepositioning' activities.

attack_patterns.name: Registry Run Keys / Startup Folder
attack_patterns.description: Adversaries establish persistence by creating registry run keys, a method of maintaining dormant footholds in critical infrastructure networks.

attack_patterns.name: Windows Command Shell
attack_patterns.description: Adversaries use the Windows Command Shell to execute commands, such as 'net use', for lateral movement and network discovery as part of living-off-the-land techniques.

attack_patterns.name: Application Layer Protocol
attack_patterns.description: Adversaries use unexpected services or protocols, such as those associated with the Fax service, for command and control communications to evade detection.

tools.name: Net
tools.description: The 'net.exe' utility was used with the 'use' command to access IPC shares, indicating lateral movement or discovery activity.

identities.name: UK Critical National Infrastructure Operators
identities.description: The collective group of organizations operating UK's critical national infrastructure, identified as primary targets for state-sponsored cyber attacks.

identities.name: National Cyber Security Centre
identities.description: The UK's national technical authority for cyber security, which issued the warning about state-sponsored attacks on CNI.

indicators.name: Suspicious Net Use for IPC Share Access
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Net Use for IPC Share Access
id: 2b3c4d5e-6f7a-4b8c-9d0e-1f2a3b4c5d6e
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of 'net.exe' to connect to the IPC$ share on a remote system, a common technique for lateral movement and discovery.
references:
  - https://www.rusi.org/news-and-comment/rusi-news/hostile-states-behind-75percent-cyber-attacks-uk-infrastructure-ncsc-ceo
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-18
tags:
  - attack.t1059.003
  - attack.execution
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\net.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'use'
      - 'IPC$'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the use of 'net.exe' to connect to the IPC$ share on a remote system, a common technique for lateral movement and discovery.

indicators.name: Persistence via HKLM Run Key
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Persistence via HKLM Run Key Modification
id: 7a8b9c0d-1e2f-4a5b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects modification of the HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run registry key, a common persistence technique used to execute malware at startup.
references:
  - https://www.rusi.org/news-and-comment/rusi-news/hostile-states-behind-75percent-cyber-attacks-uk-infrastructure-ncsc-ceo
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-18
tags:
  - attack.t1547.001
  - attack.persistence
level: high
logsource:
  category: registry_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetObject|endswith: '\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects modification of the HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run registry key, a common persistence technique used to execute malware at startup.

indicators.name: Network Connection from Unused Fax Service
indicators.pattern:
title: Network Connection from Unused Fax Service
id: 3d4e5f6a-7b8c-4d9e-af01-234567890123
status: experimental
description: Detects outbound network connections from the Fax service (fxssvc.exe), which is often disabled and can indicate misuse by an adversary for C2 or exfiltration.
references:
  - https://www.rusi.org/news-and-comment/rusi-news/hostile-states-behind-75percent-cyber-attacks-uk-infrastructure-ncsc-ceo
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-18
tags:
  - attack.t1071
  - attack.command_and_control
level: medium
logsource:
  category: network_connection
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\fxssvc.exe'
    Initiated: true
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects outbound network connections from the Fax service (fxssvc.exe), which is often disabled and can indicate misuse by an adversary for C2 or exfiltration.

relationships.source_name: Suspicious Net Use for IPC Share Access
Network Connection from Unused Fax Service
